**Secondary:** Event Planning & Volunteer Coordination

## Key Themes

1. **FOHI NYC Survey Results** — 55/800 responses analyzed; edtech and consultants are largest segments; demand for happy hours, hackathons, panels, co-working, AI-focused events
2. **2026 Event Calendar Planning** — February happy hour (V to lead), March hackathon exploration, April debate partnership with Unmuted, July NYC Summit (Starbridge venue in discussion), December holiday gathering
3. **Volunteer Program Design** — Three tiers: Event Greeters/Facilitators (Andrew Young model), Connectors (virtual office hours / expertise matching), Neighborhood Ambassadors (localized coffee/co-working)
4. **Sponsorship Strategy** — Major sponsorship goal for the year; partner list from past events; outreach-heavy Q1
5. **Distributed Leadership Model** — Low-tech, decentralized approach; hand off last-mile execution to volunteers; work within Ray/Jake's constraint of minimal new tooling (Google Sheets, Slack, email only)

## Organizations Mentioned

- **FOHI** — Future of Higher Education Initiative (parent org)
- **Unmuted / FYI** — Discussion/social club for politically curious; potential April debate co-host
- **Starbridge** — Potential venue partner for July NYC Summit
- **Modern Day** — Nonprofit Anna has started talking to for partnership
- **Andrew Young (org/events)** — Referenced as model for volunteer facilitation ("fellows" approach)
- **Harvard iLab** — Referenced by V as model for expertise office hours

## Meeting Sentiment

**Tone:** Energized, collaborative, efficient. Anna ran a tight agenda; V was enthusiastic and receptive. Strong mutual trust evident — Anna immediately delegating meaningful ownership rather than easing in gradually.

**Key Quote:** *"I trust you got a good handful of yourself and I trust your capability. So surprise me."* — Anna Bao
